message:
fix: governor grep pipefail hang + token-collector file write (#64)

Governor optimize_model_assignment() aborts under set -euo pipefail
when model state files don't exist on first run — grep returns non-zero
through the pipeline. Add || true to both prev_backend/prev_model reads.

Token-collector now writes output JSON to file (TOKEN_OUTPUT_FILE env
or /var/run/hive-metrics/tokens.json) so governor can read weekly/hourly
budget data without depending on dashboard server stdout capture.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -508,8 +508,8 @@ optimize_model_assignment() {
     local cost_weight
     cost_weight=$(get_cost_weight "$backend" "$model")
     local prev_backend prev_model
-    prev_backend=$(grep '^BACKEND=' "$STATE_DIR/model_${agent}" 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2)
-    prev_model=$(grep '^MODEL=' "$STATE_DIR/model_${agent}" 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2)
+    prev_backend=$(grep '^BACKEND=' "$STATE_DIR/model_${agent}" 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || true)
+    prev_model=$(grep '^MODEL=' "$STATE_DIR/model_${agent}" 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2 || true)
 
     cat > "$STATE_DIR/model_${agent}" <<MODELEOF
 BACKEND=$backend

@@ -262,5 +262,11 @@ result = {
     },
 }
 
-print(json.dumps(result))
+output = json.dumps(result)
+print(output)
+try:
+    with open(os.environ.get('TOKEN_OUTPUT_FILE', '/var/run/hive-metrics/tokens.json'), 'w') as f:
+        f.write(output)
+except (OSError, IOError):
+    pass
 PYEOF
